metadata.summary: Pass previous_response_id on Responses API to preserve reasoning items between turns. Avoids reconstruction of Chat-Completions message arrays.

content.title: previous_response_id — Reasoning-Item Reuse on the Responses API
content.summary: When chaining turns of an OpenAI reasoning model (o3, o4-mini, gpt-5) on the Responses API, pass previous_response_id=<prior.id> instead of reconstructing a Chat-Completions–style message array. The Responses API keeps reasoning items adjacent to their function calls server-side, keyed by id; supplying the id reattaches them on the next turn. This preserves the model's mid-loop "thinking", lifts cache-hit rate, and cuts both latency and tokens. Under ZDR (store=false), use the encrypted-content variant instead — previous_response_id is silently ignored.

why.p: Reasoning items are not just chat messages — they are intermediate scratchpad tokens the model produced between tool calls. The legacy approach (Chat Completions) flattens them away and the model "thinks from scratch" on every turn, producing a 20-40% regression on agentic benchmarks (OpenAI cookbook + community measurements). The Responses API was specifically designed to keep these items co-located with the call/response pair they justify; opting in via previous_response_id is the only supported way to inherit them on the next turn for stateful (store=true) deployments.

list.item: Multi-turn agent loops on o3, o4-mini, or gpt-5 where the model calls tools and you feed results back.
Conversational reasoning agents where consecutive user turns continue the same task.
Long planning loops where mid-trajectory thoughts inform later decisions.
Anywhere you previously paid for "re-thinking" by reconstructing message arrays manually.
Non-reasoning models (gpt-4.1, gpt-4.1-mini) — there are no reasoning items to reuse.
ZDR / store=false deployments — the id is silently ignored; use include=["reasoning.encrypted_content"] and round-trip the blob (see encrypted-reasoning pattern).
First turn of a session — there is no prior id yet; pass the user message normally.
Cross-session continuation — reasoning items expire; do not assume yesterday's id still resolves.

section.name: Chain reasoning turns by id, not by message array
section.p: On the Responses API, pass previous_response_id to inherit reasoning items between turns; Chat-Completions–style message reconstruction drops them.

rule.statement: For multi-turn loops on a reasoning model (o3, o4-mini, gpt-5) using the Responses API with default storage, every turn after the first MUST set previous_response_id=<prior.id> and pass ONLY the new input items (user message, tool results) — never reconstruct the full message history manually.
rule.rationale: The Responses API stores reasoning items adjacent to their function calls server-side; previous_response_id is the documented mechanism to reattach them. Reconstructing messages from .output drops the reasoning blocks (the SDK silently strips them in Chat-Completions format), and the model loses 20-40% on agentic benchmarks because each turn re-derives plans it had already produced.

example.code: r1 = client.responses.create( model="o4-mini", input=user_msg, tools=tools, ) # … application executes the tool call … r2 = client.responses.create( model="o4-mini", previous_response_id=r1.id, input=[ {"type": "function_call_output", "call_id": call_id, "output": tool_result} ], )
example.note: Only the new tool result is passed in; reasoning items from r1 are reattached server-side via the id.

example.code: # Anti-pattern: rebuild a message array every turn. messages = [{"role": "user", "content": user_msg}] r1 = client.responses.create(model="o4-mini", input=messages, tools=tools) messages.extend([m for m in r1.output if m.type != "reasoning"]) # drops reasoning messages.append({"type": "function_call_output", "call_id": ..., "output": ...}) r2 = client.responses.create(model="o4-mini", input=messages)
example.note: The filter strips reasoning items; even without the explicit filter, manual message reconstruction is documented to lose them.

section.p: Reasoning models emit a hidden chain of thought (the "reasoning" item type) between user input and final output, including between tool calls. Those items are not chat messages — they are scratchpad tokens the model produced to justify the next action. They cannot be reproduced from text alone.

antipattern.description: Treating .output items like chat completions and storing only the assistant text plus tool calls.
antipattern.reason: Reasoning items are the part the model needs to continue its thought; without them every turn restarts cognition.

section.reference: Reference loop showing both stored and ZDR variants.
section.link: OpenAI cookbook — reasoning items in the Responses API.
OpenAI docs — conversation state via previous_response_id.
Migrate from Chat Completions to Responses.

section.name: ZDR fallback — encrypted reasoning content
section.p: Under store=false, previous_response_id is silently ignored; round-trip include=["reasoning.encrypted_content"] yourself.

rule.statement: When the request has store=false (Zero Data Retention), set include=["reasoning.encrypted_content"] on every Responses call and append the encrypted reasoning items from the prior response into the next call's input array; do not pass previous_response_id — it is ignored without a server-side store.
rule.rationale: ZDR contractually disallows server-side persistence, so the API has nothing to look up by id. The encrypted-content opt-in returns reasoning items as opaque blobs that the next call decrypts in-process only — preserving reasoning continuity without retaining anything at rest.

example.note: Encrypted blobs are passed back literally; the server decrypts in-memory and reattaches to the new turn.

section.p: Encrypted reasoning items are larger than their unencrypted counterparts. Expect ~5-15% input-token overhead vs the stored variant.

antipattern.description: Using encrypted-content round-trip when the deployment does NOT have a ZDR requirement.
antipattern.reason: Pays the encryption tax for no compliance benefit; previous_response_id is cheaper and equivalent for stored deployments.

antipattern.description: Passing previous_response_id with store=false and assuming reasoning carries forward.
antipattern.reason: The id resolves to nothing because nothing was stored; the call succeeds but reasoning is lost — silent regression on quality.

section.link: OpenAI community thread — encrypted_content with store=false.

template.purpose: Working agent loop using previous_response_id plus a ZDR fallback branch.
